heading: About This Task
body:
This quick start describes how to load sample documents that contain
vector embeddings into your cluster, create a MongoDB Vector Search index on those
embeddings, and then perform semantic search to return documents that
are similar to your query.

*Time required: 15 minutes*

In this quick start, you complete the following steps:

- Create an index definition for the
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection that indexes the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field as the `vector` type. The
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field contains embeddings created
  using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The index
  definition specifies `2048` vector dimensions and measures
  similarity using `dotProduct`.

- Run a MongoDB Vector Search query that searches the sample
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection. The query uses the
  [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) stage to search the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field, which contains embeddings
  created using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The
  query searches the `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field using
  vector embeddings for the string *time travel*. It considers up to
  `150` nearest neighbors, and returns `10` documents in the
  results.

To learn more, see [Learning Summary](avs-qs-learning-summary).

This quick start describes how to create a local Atlas deployment
with sample data loaded into the deployment, create a MongoDB Vector Search index for
the sample data, and then perform semantic search to return documents
that are similar to your query.

*Time required: 15 minutes*

In this quick start, you complete the following steps:

- Deploy Atlas locally via Atlas CLI with sample data loaded.

  To create a local Atlas deployment using Docker commands, see
  [Create a Local Atlas Deployment with Docker](atlas/cli/atlas-cli-deploy-docker).

- Create an index definition for the
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection that indexes the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field as the `vector` type. The
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field contains embeddings created
  using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The index
  definition specifies `2048` vector dimensions and measures
  similarity using `dotProduct`.

- Run a MongoDB Vector Search query that searches the sample
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection. The query uses the
  [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) stage to search the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field, which contains embeddings
  created using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The
  query searches the `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field using
  vector embeddings for the string *time travel*. It considers up to
  `150` nearest neighbors, and returns `10` documents in the
  results.

To learn more, see [Learning Summary](avs-qs-learning-summary).

Before you begin, you must download the following:

- Docker

- Atlas CLI v5.3.1 or later

- [mongosh](mongodb-shell)

This quick start describes how to create a local Atlas deployment
with sample data loaded into the deployment, create a MongoDB Vector Search index for
the sample data, and then perform semantic search to return documents
that are similar to your query.

*Time required: 15 minutes*

In this quick start, you complete the following steps:

- Deploy Atlas locally via Atlas CLI with sample data loaded.

  To create a local Atlas deployment using Docker commands, see
  [Create a Local Atlas Deployment with Docker](atlas/cli/atlas-cli-deploy-docker).

- Create an index definition for the
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection that indexes the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field as the `vector` type. The
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field contains embeddings created
  using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The index
  definition specifies `2048` vector dimensions and measures
  similarity using `dotProduct`.

- Run a MongoDB Vector Search query that searches the sample
  `sample_mflix.embedded_movies` collection. The query uses the
  [$vectorSearch](pipe.$vectorSearch) stage to search the
  `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field, which contains embeddings
  created using Voyage AI's `voyage-3-large` embedding model. The
  query searches the `plot_embedding_voyage_3_large` field using
  vector embeddings for the string *time travel*. It considers up to
  `150` nearest neighbors, and returns `10` documents in the
  results.

To learn more, see [Learning Summary](avs-qs-learning-summary).

Before you begin, you must have the following:

- Docker

- Atlas CLI v5.3.1 or later

- [mongosh](mongodb-shell)

- Voyage AI API (Application Programming Interface) keys created from the Atlas UI

  To learn more about creating a Voyage AI API (Application Programming Interface) key from the
  Atlas UI, see [using](voyageai/management/api-keys).

  > **Note:** Local Atlas deployment doesn't support Voyage AI API (Application Programming Interface) keys
  > created directly from Voyage AI.

This quick start demonstrates how to deploy MongoDB Search and MongoDB Vector Search in Docker,
load sample data into the MongoDB cluster, create a vector search index
for the sample data, and run vector search queries against the sample data.

*Time required: 20 minutes*

The quick start walks you through the following steps:

- Deploy MongoDB and `mongot` in Docker.

  For more details on deploying by using a tarball or as an image in Docker,
  see [Install MongoDB Community and MongoDB Search](manual/administration/install-community).

- Load sample data, create a vector search index, and run vector search queries on your local deployment.

> **Note:** The resulting set up of this quick start is not secure. For
> more information on securing your deployment, see [Security for
> Self-Managed Deployments](manual/administration/security-checklist).

This tutorial creates the following architecture:

- A MongoDB Community Edition Server (`mongod`) with a single node replica set
  on port 27017

- A MongoDB Search (`mongot`) search engine component on port 27028

- Persistant data volumes on both ports

- Pre-loaded sample data

> **Note:** For a production deployment, MongoDB recommends that you use a replica
> set with at least three members.

Before you begin, you must complete the following prerequisites:

- Download Docker v4.40 or higher

- Download Docker Compose

- Download the `curl` command

- Download `mongosh` locally or have access to it through Docker

- Create Voyage AI API (Application Programming Interface) keys from the [using](voyageai/management/api-keys)
  or directly from 

  We recommend two separate keys to enable MongoDB Vector Search to [How to Automatically Generate Embeddings](avs-auto-embeddings) at index- and query-time.

> **Note:** Your provider endpoint for generating embeddings depends on
> whether you create the API (Application Programming Interface) key from the Atlas UI or
> directly from Voyage AI.
